back_translation: Behold, the hope of capturing him is false; the mere sight of him is overwhelming.
No one is fierce enough to rouse him. Then who is able to stand before Me?
Who has given Me anything beforehand, that I should repay him? Everything under heaven is Mine.
I will not keep silent about his limbs, his power, and his graceful form.
Who can strip off his outer covering? Who can approach him with a bridle?
Who can open his jaws? His rows of teeth are terrifying.
His rows of scales are his pride, sealed together tightly like a seal.
Each scale is so close to another that no air can pass between them.
They cling to one another, locked together so they cannot be separated.
When he sneezes, light flashes out, and his eyes are like the rays of dawn.
Firebrands stream from his mouth; sparks of fire shoot out.
Smoke pours from his nostrils, like a boiling pot over burning reeds.
His breath kindles coals, and flames pour out of his mouth.
Strength resides in his neck, and terror leaps ahead of him.
The folds of his flesh cling together, firm and unmovable.
His chest is hard as stone, as hard as a lower millstone.
When he rises up, the mighty are afraid; they retreat because of his thrashing.
The sword that strikes him does no harm, nor does spear, dart, or arrow.
He regards iron as mere straw and bronze as rotten wood.
Arrows cannot make him flee; slingstones become like chaff to him.
A club is regarded as straw, and he laughs at the whir of the lance.
His underside is sharp like potsherds; he drags through the mud like a threshing sledge.
He makes the deep boil like a pot and stirs the sea like a jar of ointment.
He leaves a shining wake behind him, so that one would think the deep had white hair.
Nothing on earth is his equal; he is a creature without fear.
He looks down with scorn on all that is lofty; he is king over all the proud creatures.'
